does: This paper introduces the Safety-Aware Denoiser (SAD), an inference-time safety framework for text diffusion models that steers the iterative denoising process toward provably safe regions of the text space. Rather than modifying the underlying model through retraining, SAD integrates safety constraints directly into the denoising steps, guiding the model away from unsafe outputs. The authors evaluate SAD across multiple safety dimensions including hazard taxonomy compliance, memorization risks, and jailbreak resilience. Their experiments demonstrate that SAD substantially reduces unsafe generations while maintaining generation quality and diversity without requiring expensive model retraining.
connects: This work extends the nascent but growing literature on safety in diffusion models, building on recent advances showing that text diffusion models offer viable alternatives to autoregressive generation. It directly responds to the limitation that existing safety techniques—post-hoc filtering, inference-time constraints, and safety training approaches like RLHF—were developed primarily for autoregressive architectures and prove inadequate for diffusion models' unique iterative generation process. SAD relates to broader work on controllable text generation and safety guidance methods, incorporating ideas about steering generation toward constrained regions while remaining computationally lightweight. The approach complements recent efforts to understand and mitigate risks in diffusion models.
enables: This work opens several promising directions for safer text generation systems. The lightweight, retraining-free nature of SAD enables rapid deployment of safety updates and flexible customization of safety constraints without model modifications, supporting practical real-world deployment. The framework establishes a foundation for more sophisticated safety-guidance techniques in diffusion models, potentially inspiring methods that combine multiple safety objectives or adapt constraints dynamically based on context. Future work could extend SAD toward other modalities, explore tighter integration of safety constraints with generation objectives, and investigate how diffusion-based approaches might offer inherent safety advantages over autoregressive models when properly constrained. The provable safety guarantees hint at possibilities for formal verification of generation safety.
